title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
summary: Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq, discusses his hardware chip designed specifically for LLM inference. Groq entered a $20 billion strategic agreement with NVIDIA and is presented alongside discussion of inference optimization techniques, cost-per-token efficiency, and the Jevons Paradox effect on compute demand.
writeup: Julia Turc interviews Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq, about the company's hardware chip built specifically for LLM inference rather than general-purpose computation. The conversation covers inference optimization techniques, cost-per-token economics, and a $20 billion strategic agreement between Groq and NVIDIA. Ross frames rising compute demand through the Jevons Paradox: as inference gets cheaper and more efficient, total consumption grows rather than shrinks. Additional topics include local inference, scheduling, Mixture of Experts, auto-regressive generation, and diffusion models.
